g.data-narration: Welcome to polynomials of matrices. We'll explore how polynomials apply to square matrices, the Cayley-Hamilton theorem, and important practical applications.
A polynomial of a matrix substitutes the matrix into a scalar polynomial. For p(x) = aв‚Ђ + aв‚Ѓx + aв‚‚xВІ + ... + aв‚™xвЃї, we get p(A) = aв‚ЂI + aв‚ЃA + aв‚‚AВІ + ... + aв‚™AвЃї.
g.text: Given p(x) = aв‚Ђ + aв‚Ѓx + aв‚‚xВІ + ... + aв‚™xвЃї and nГ—n matrix A:
Example: p(x) = 2 + 3x - xВІ в†’ p(A) = 2I + 3A - AВІ

g.data-narration: The characteristic polynomial of an nГ—n matrix A is p(О») = det(A - О»I). For 2Г—2 matrices, p(О») = О»ВІ - trace(A)О» + det(A).
g.text: For nГ—n matrix A: p(О») = det(A - О»I) = (-1)вЃїО»вЃї + cв‚™в‚‹в‚ЃО»вЃївЃ»В№ + ... + cв‚ЃО» + cв‚Ђ

g.data-narration: The Cayley-Hamilton theorem states every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation: p(A) = 0. This fundamental result connects matrices to their polynomials.
g.text: Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic polynomial:
If p(О») = det(A - О»I), then substituting A gives zero matrix
For 2Г—2: AВІ - trace(A)A + det(A)I = 0 в†’ AВІ = trace(A)A - det(A)I

g.data-narration: The minimal polynomial is the monic polynomial of least degree that annihilates the matrix. It divides the characteristic polynomial and has the same distinct roots.
g.text: The minimal polynomial m(x) of matrix A is the monic polynomial of least degree such that m(A) = 0.
вЂў It divides the characteristic polynomial: m(x) | p(x)
вЂў They share the same distinct roots (eigenvalues)
вЂў Degree of minimal polynomial в‰¤ n (matrix order), equals size of largest Jordan block

g.data-narration: Using Cayley-Hamilton, we can find matrix inverse. From p(A) = (-1)вЃїAвЃї + cв‚™в‚‹в‚ЃAвЃївЃ»В№ + ... + cв‚ЃA + cв‚ЂI = 0, rearrange to get AвЃ»В№ = -(1/cв‚Ђ)[(-1)вЃїAвЃївЃ»В№ + cв‚™в‚‹в‚ЃAвЃївЃ»ВІ + ... + cв‚ЃI].
g.text: From p(A) = 0, rearrange for AвЃ»В№ (when det(A) в‰ 0):

g.data-narration: CH theorem lets us compute high powers like AВ№вЃ°вЃ° by reducing them to combinations of I, A, AВІ, ..., AвЃївЃ»В№. For 2Г—2, use AВІ = trace(A)A - det(A)I repeatedly.
g.text: Express higher powers of A in terms of I, A, AВІ, ..., AвЃївЃ»В№ using CH theorem:
This method efficiently computes AВ№вЃ°вЃ° by reducing to linear combination of I and A
